title: Image of a union is the union of images
informal_statement: For every function f : alpha -> beta and sets s, t : Set alpha: the image of the union f '' (s union t) equals the union of the images f '' s union f '' t.
informal_proof_idea: Cited directly via Mathlib's Set.image_union. A hand-unfolded set-extensionality proof (ext y; constructor; rintro/rcases on each direction) was attempted first but hit a tactic-transport hazard: nested `·` bullet blocks lost their structure under this environment's proof transport (both flat_tactic_sequence and a naive raw_lean_block attempt failed with different parse/scope errors), so the direct citation was used instead as the robust, transport-safe route.
formal_statement_pp: theorem image_union : ∀ {α β : Type} (f : α → β) (s t : Set α), f '' (s ∪ t) = f '' s ∪ f '' t

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 5ca732f2), kernel_verified on the third Solve attempt. Steps 1-2: a hand-unfolded set-extensionality proof with nested `·` bullets kernel-failed twice, once under default flat_tactic_sequence transport (bullet structure lost, 'Unknown identifier hx') and once under raw_lean_block (a leading-indentation mismatch produced 'introN failed: no additional binders'). Step 3 switched to directly citing Set.image_union, which kernel-verified cleanly. Fills the 'image/preimage' gap explicitly named in packets/elementary/functions' README stated focus but never covered by a prior packet; closes the corresponding backlog item in packets/elementary/functions/QUEUE.md.
